name: the-rise-and-fall-of-imperial-china
description: Yuhua Wang's groundbreaking study of Chinese state development over two millennia. A social network approach to understanding why some Chinese dynasties produced strong states with short-lived rulers while others produced weak states with long-lived emperors.
useCase: Understand the deep structural forces that shaped Chinese imperial history and their implications for state development in the modern world.
knownRisks: This is an academic work (Princeton University Press) requiring familiarity with social science concepts.
The elite social terrain framework is a simplification of complex historical processes.
The book focuses on structural factors (elite networks) and may underemphasize contingency and individual agency.
